summary: DVLT is a held position that the bot's own research journal already lists as 'avoid' for serial dilution, yet exposure continues: a fresh Maxim ATM was signed June 2026 on top of a $1B shelf, and a non-binding $2B structured-financing term sheet could hand board control to a counterparty and dilute >50% of voting stock.

UUUU.note: $300M shelf + active ATM; issued 3.04M shares for $54.4M in Q1 2026 (244.4M shares out, ~+20% YoY) — real ongoing dilution, though funded by solid uranium fundamentals and the Vacuumschmelze deal, not distress.

DVLT.note: Serial diluter (855M shares out, +1518% YoY per prior research); $1B S-3 effective 3/25/26, $60M offering closed, NEW Maxim ATM signed June 2026, plus a non-binding term sheet for up to $2B in tranches priced $1.55-2.00/share that would cede board control and dilute >50% of voting stock if fully drawn. Already on the bot's own avoid list; entry catalyst (meme-coin/tokenization hype) has no fundamental support.

CAI.note: IPO lockup (expired ~Dec 2025) already passed without incident; company just authorized a $100M buyback (anti-dilutive), no new secondary or shelf activity found.

AVO.note: Pending Calavo Growers acquisition is REAL and progressing (S-4 filed 3/9/26, HSR cleared 4/17/26) — AVO will issue ~0.979 new shares per Calavo share plus cash, mechanical dilution for existing holders, plus deal-completion risk. Contradicts intel.json's dismissal of this as search-engine noise.

NTSK.note: Ticker ambiguity resolved: broker price ($12.44-12.80 avg/current) matches Netskope Inc (NASDAQ:NTSK) almost exactly, not NetSol (NTWK). Netskope is a $5B cybersecurity company with a director/backer buying $7.2M on 7/8 — fundamentally sound, but off the small-cap thesis this bot is built around.

flags.why: Serapha Bio reverse merger confirmed: pro forma existing BOLD holders retain only ~3.69% of the combined company (Serapha gets ~96.31%), plus a reverse split and authorized-share increase are conditions of closing. The +61.7% premarket pop is the classic 'sell the news' distressed reverse-merger dynamic, not a buyable catalyst.

Top radar candidate (5.93) but carries an active $100M ATM sales agreement since 2024 plus a Feb-2026 $100M underwritten offering (20M shares at $5) that is now underwater (stock $3.75) — dilution is live, not hypothetical.

Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 1/13/26 already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM program — ongoing drip-dilution setup. Currently below buy threshold (3.33) so moot for now, but flag if score rises.

dilution_watch.note: Signed non-binding term sheet for up to $2.0B structured financing in four $500M tranches at $1.55-2.00/share; full draw implies >50% dilution and board-control shift to counterparty.
New Maxim Group co-sales-agent ATM agreement signed; Maxim also received 2.73M warrants at $0.6325 strike — fresh drip-dilution mechanism on top of the $1B shelf.
$100M underwritten offering, 20M shares at $5.00; stock now trades below that price at $3.75. $100M ATM sales agreement (since 2024) remains active.
Merger agreement with Serapha Bio; S-4 pending, reverse split + authorized-share increase required to close; existing holders diluted to ~3.69% pro forma.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM program active to fund natural-gas expansion.
3.04M shares issued for $54.4M under existing $300M shelf; share count 244.4M, up ~20% YoY.
S-4 for Calavo Growers acquisition (announced 1/14/26, HSR cleared 4/17/26); new AVO shares (0.979/Calavo share) to be issued to Calavo holders at closing.

disagreements: intel.json dismissed the Mission Produce/Calavo Growers acquisition as a 'recurring search-engine mixup' with no primary-source confirmation — SEC filings confirm it is a real, progressing deal (S-4 filed 3/9/26, HSR cleared 4/17/26, announced 1/14/26). Treat the pending share issuance and deal-completion risk as real, not noise.

intel.json carries no alert on DVLT this snapshot despite it being a held position with an active new ATM (signed June 2026) and a pending $2B financing that could cede board control — this deserves an active warning every cycle it remains held, not silence.

intel.json flags NTSK as an unresolved 'ticker ambiguity' — the broker's quoted price matches Netskope (NASDAQ:NTSK) almost exactly, resolving the identity. Worth noting the held position is a $5B cybersecurity company, not a small-cap, which is off-thesis even though fundamentally it looks fine.
